notes: OCHA reported 23 Palestinians killed, 1 body retrieved, 1 person died of wounds, and 112 injured between 24 and 30 June, citing MoH figures.

location: Al Mawasi area of Khan Younis
notes: OCHA reported that a mother and her baby daughter were killed in an airstrike in Al Mawasi; the strike reportedly destroyed tents for more than 150 families and damaged those of an additional 250 families.

location: near a temporary education learning space
notes: OCHA reported that a fifth-grade child died from injuries sustained during an airstrike on 23 June near a temporary education learning space.
OCHA reported that Israeli forces killed three Palestinians, including one child, during raids and search operations in the West Bank between 23 and 29 June; about 20 Palestinians were injured.

location: eastern Deir al Balah governorate and eastern Gaza city
notes: OCHA reported that more than 20 families were displaced from eastern Deir al Balah governorate and more than a dozen from eastern Gaza city between 26 and 27 June after people were ordered to leave or fled as forces advanced.

location: Wadi Salman Bedouin community in Area C of Ramallah governorate
notes: OCHA reported that a Palestinian Bedouin household of eight people, including four children and three women, was displaced from Wadi Salman following repeated settler attacks and relocated to a safer area.
OCHA reported that Israeli authorities demolished 12 livelihood structures in Shu'fat Camp, affecting 12 households comprising 48 people and damaging commercial shops.

location: Gaza Strip - 130 health facilities across territory
notes: OCHA reported that in just two weeks, over 9,000 cases of chickenpox were recorded across more than 130 health facilities in Gaza, with over half in Khan Younis, driven by deteriorating environmental conditions and overcrowding.

OCHA reported a critical disruption in haemodialysis services at Al Shifa Hospital due to shortage of sodium bicarbonate, reducing treatment capacity for approximately 240 patients with end-stage kidney disease and requiring reduction of dialysis sessions.

OCHA reported that between January 2023 and 29 June 2026, more than 6,200 Palestinians (including over 3,000 children) have been displaced due to settler attacks and access restrictions, with over 2,300 people in 2026 alone.

location: Al Bowereh and Hiwara communities in Hebron governorate
notes: OCHA documented that establishment of settlement outposts near Al Bowereh (120+ families) and Hiwara (30 households) was accompanied by repeated settler attacks on homes, agricultural land, and livelihood assets, with at least 6 documented attacks in Al Bowereh and 11 in Hiwara.

OCHA reported that Israeli authorities demolished a multi-storey Palestinian-owned building in Kafr Aqab on 22 June, displacing two households and destroying property on the 'West Bank' side of the Barrier.

location: East Jerusalem areas West Bank side of Barrier
notes: OCHA documented that in 2026, 37% of structures demolished in East Jerusalem for lacking Israeli-issued building permits were located on the West Bank side of the Barrier, nearly double the six-year average of 19%.

location: Kafr ad Dik in Salfit governorate
notes: OCHA documented that on 28 June, settlers established a new tent and threatened five Palestinian herding families, prompting them to relocate approximately 400 sheep and other belongings.

OCHA reported that Israeli settlers cut electricity cables connected to an under-construction school and broke into several agricultural structures in Rammun, stealing farming equipment in an area where a nearby settlement outpost has restricted Palestinian farmers' access to agricultural land.
OCHA documented that on 29 June, Israeli settlers occupied an unfinished Palestinian house in Jalud village.

location: Khan al Ahmar in Jerusalem governorate
notes: OCHA reported that residents in Khan al Ahmar reported near-daily settler incursions involving damage to water and electricity infrastructure, harassment, and grazing of livestock on community land.

location: East Jerusalem - Kafr Aqab and Shu'fat Camp
notes: OCHA reported that 14 Palestinian-owned structures were demolished during the reporting period for lacking Israeli-issued building permits, including 12 in East Jerusalem and 2 in Area C, affecting multiple households.

location: UNRWA Kalandia Training Centre (KTC) in East Jerusalem
notes: OCHA reported that on 30 June, Israeli forces forcibly entered the UNRWA Kalandia Training Centre, inspected premises, and informed staff that the vocational training facility providing free education to hundreds of students would be required to close; the UN condemned the breach of inviolability.
